reviews.review_title: A lot of fun, needs updates.
reviews.review_description: I enjoy this workout a lot. The demons don’t scare me the way other boxing type monster work outs do. It’s cartoonish and fun. I just wish they’d update it to add more variety of dialog and things like that.

I LOVE this so far. I just did the trial, but based on it, this is awesome. Most boxing games are so dull, but this one is different. Combining fantasy and fitness is a tough line to walk, but these folks succeeded. If they stay focused on developing this, I'd be willing to bet it will become one of the most popular fitness programs in VR. Thank You! I've been looking for something unique.
Game is super fun but not much to it. like 5 levels. Not as fun if there was more to the overall levels.
My expectations that I would enjoy the game was close to zero but actually I do a lot. Gives you solid exercise and also is funny. Recommended.

It’s a fun story, good voice work, but the action isn’t for me. Dozens of quick time events assault you all at once, and even though it adjusts to your height, the portal to hell doesn’t, so a very tall person can’t see what’s coming. I’ll stick with FitXR.
I could get a sweat going but things like sounds and moves get repetitive

reviews.review_title: Great start; not enough content/too easy
reviews.review_description:
I really like the idea but to be honest I was a little underwhelmed by the limited content.  I see only three levels, with three difficulties each.  There needs to be a LOT more variety to get into the league of the heavyweight exercise apps.

It's also too easy for now; even on highest setting, it barely gets my heart rate to 100.  I suggest 1) many more attacks you have to dodge; 2) your punch force mattering for damage; 3) more variety of enemies with more difficult attack sequences; 4) needing to know which hand and which direction is punching; 5) time limits when you are punching them; 6) multiple enemies attacking at once asynchronously (that's prob a harder request).

I also think the trainers should be more involved and fight alongside you.  It's weird they just stand there.  So a lot of room to improve; will increase my rating as the app gets better!

reviews.review_title: Nice little game with room to grow!
reviews.review_description:
It is a nice concept but becomes repetitive soon.
For me it is a pass for now (will request refund) but I will keep it in my watch list and may repurchase if I see some of the improvements I would like implemented.
My thoughts to the developers:
a) implement more workouts of different varieties 
b) would be great if there is some sense of progression by introducing tougher challenges AFTER defeating the previous easier ones. I do not want to have flat access to every workout but a path of progression where I have to earn my way to the tougher ones. You can implement an optional unlock for those who prefer to access everything, but make it an option for the player.
c) When you present those timed targets, with the declining circle that you have to hit at the right time, DO NOT IGNORE THE EARLY HITS ON THE TARGETS. When you hit a target and see your fist go through it without any feedback, only for it to get destroyed 1 sec later when you hand is maybe trying to strike again or pull back is very immersion breaking! Yes, I understand that you want to time the hit, but hitting air with no feedback is annoying. You can still register the hit as an early / invalid strike, destroy the target with a different sound and do not count for the score (even count as negative if you prefer)... but do not let the hand go through the target without any feedback...

Ah, Exercise Your Demons. Novel idea of a fitness game. I very much wanted to enjoy this title more than I ultimately did.  Despite the cool setting which sees the protagonist working out in the fiery pits of hell, the player is tortured by a lack of engaging combat and repeated scenarios. There's a handful of critters to beat up but aside from aerial combatants, the way in which you interact with them is by dodging and hitting punching bags/weights. This immediately creates a bit of a disconnect between the player and game. Further complicating matters are the lack of varied backgrounds and arenas. The cell shaded art does a wonderful job of providing cues and whimsy but again, can only do so much of the heavy lifting. 

I rather enjoyed the banter between the trainers and the opportunity to level up gear/relationships, but understand that your mileage may very based on the style of humor. Some may very well find it grating. Audio is a strange bag as well….at times the trainers would talk over themselves or sound as if their voice lines were compressed to high hell. Zephyr, the chill laid back option of a companion would have been perfect for leading you through Yoga lessons and finding your inner peace. Unfortunately, this low hanging fruit was missed and the only gameplay to await your d@mned soul will be more punching and more dodging.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share such a detailed and thoughtful review of Exercise Your Demons! We genuinely appreciate your candid feedback about both the game's strengths and areas for improvement. Your observations about the combat variety, arena diversity, and audio issues are particularly valuable, and we're actively working to address these aspects of the game.

We're glad you enjoyed the cell-shaded art style and character interactions - these were important design pillars for us. Your suggestion about expanding Zephyr's role to include yoga and meditation is especially intriguing...

We have a new feature we're working on for a future update that's less gameplay focused and more exercise/mindfulness.. eager to share more details soon

While we can't share specifics just yet, we want you to know that we're committed to expanding and enriching the EYD experience. If you see the potential in what we've created, we encourage you to stay tuned – we're just getting started, and your feedback will help shape the game's evolution.

Keep an eye on our social channels for updates on what's next for Exercise Your Demons!

reviews.review_title: Nice art style, maybe good for kids…
reviews.review_description: I like the art, they put a lot of work into getting the visuals right, and it shows. My biggest issue is that it doesn’t really seem to emphasize good form the way that some of the other exercise apps do - I felt like I could touch the targets in any way, from any angle, and it would register as a hit just the same. Also I found it a little unclear where/how far I was supposed to be ducking and dodging.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the thoughtful feedback! We're really glad you appreciated the visual design - our art team put their hearts into creating a distinctive style that would be both engaging and functional during workouts.
We want to clarify that the game does actually take into account both angle and velocity of your strikes, which affect your scoring and effectiveness. However, your feedback makes it clear that we need to do a better job of communicating this to players and making these mechanics more apparent during gameplay. Similarly, we hear you on making the ducking and dodging guidance clearer - this is something we're actively looking to improve.
Your point about emphasizing good form is spot-on and particularly valuable to us. We'd love to hear more about which apps you've seen handle this well! We're committed to making Exercise Your Demons not just fun, but also an effective workout tool, and we're exploring ways to better guide and encourage proper exercise form.
Keep the feedback coming - it's crucial for helping us improve the experience for all our players!

We do have a facebook group if you'd like to join!  https://www.facebook.com/groups/1147901030227377

reviews.review_description: You fight different enemies that attack you through a portal in mixed reality. This is a fun way for kids to get moving and get some light exercise. There are also different trainers that give you special abilities and a shop for some cosmetics to keep playing. This is not very deep, but cute fun for kids and kids at heart

reviews.review_title: Fun Exercise but Lack of Content. 👍👎
reviews.review_description:
The base Game is good, the Visuals are well made and mostly have Style and the Intro and first 30 Minutes are Fun are really Fun. 👍

Sadly, right then you realise there's only 3 Stages and they are basically the same and the Story Cutscenes are just boring. 
There's no new Stages to Unlock, no new Enemy Variants, no new Attack Patterns, only what you've seen in the first 30 Minutes, which are 3 Stages and some Glove-Skins and T-Shirts for your Companions.

As of now this feels very Early Access but pretty polished for that i must admit. 
It just needs more way more Content.
What is here right now is really fun, just not enough yet.
Would the Game get a big Content Update this would elevate to at least 4 out of 5.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for this thoughtful and balanced review! We completely understand your perspective on the content depth.

You're absolutely right - while we're proud of the polish and style we've achieved in the base game, we recognize that players like you are hungry for more variety. We launched with a focused set of core features to ensure everything worked smoothly, but we have bigger plans!

We're looking at potential ways to keep expanding the game with:

New stages
Additional enemy types with unique attack patterns
More attack patterns
Other kinds of workouts or activities

Your feedback about wanting more content beyond the initial 30 minutes is exactly the kind of insight that helps us prioritize our development roadmap. We're committed to growing the game beyond its current scope.

Keep an eye out for updates in the coming months - we're excited to bring you more content that matches the polish level you've already experienced. Thank you for recognizing the potential, and we hope you'll check back as we continue to expand the game!

reviews.review_description: Nice to have an exercise game that doesn't involve hitting balls. Much fun!  Great job dev :)

So, I have a history of many years in health and fitness but due to some injuries I can’t do much anymore. Apps like Supernatural and others in VR are amazing but perhaps a bit too painful for me at times so I don’t really use them. I’d often hear people talk and compare their scores in these apps and get kind of bummed out about it.But now, along comes this cute little app and let me tell you, I’m finally able to do a few things which is kinda neat! 

The game starts out really cute and as an adult not ashamed to admit, I love watching cartoons lol, I absolutely adore the set up for this thing. The fact that it is also played out as a kind of game is fun as well. 
Sit through the beginning dialogue because it is worth it. It is very cute and enjoyable and yes, I’m sure this is geared towards younger folks, but this actually works out very nicely for me. Not only do I get to do something a little physical, which is at times a little taxing, but I also get to enjoy the non-pressure, fun atmosphere of these little avatars and their sarcastic remarks, lol!
The four main characters are enjoyable, (love the Demon book that constantly talks smack at ya) and two of them actually work with you, Zephyr who is the Zen female trainer, and Ash, who is the buffed male trainer, of course, both demons.
You can choose which you work with and at a certain point you can combine their powers with yours to fight off the bad demons. 
Demons keep coming at you and instead of actually hitting them, you hit a metallic ball that appears in front of you which leads to a few shields (think boxers hitting focus gloves on their trainers hands) that you hit to finish off that particular demon.
It moves fairly fast from one to another and there are little breaks where gym equipment comes flying at you and you have to doge it but also collect some coins. This is fun and enjoyable. I would say that if they would just give you a little more room in the area where you move left,right, up, and down to avoid the equipment, that would be awesome as I feel like I have to be Slenderman in order to really avoid everything lol.
Another plus for this is that you can actually do this sitting down…sort-of…I’ve played it both ways and sitting down, of course is difficult for the in between gym equipment attacks and also when you have to move out of the way when one of the demons is trying to hit you, but in general, you can still enjoy it that way with a little practice. I think the game would be even better if they actually had a specifically seated mode that they could work out for people because there are a lot of handicapped individuals and people with injuries that could really benefit from this app and the movement they could experience with it. I was a trainer for 6 years and many of the special populations I worked with at times would be the perfect people to recommend this to. And actually, I will still recommend this to people who have to sit in order to exercise and just have them not worry so much about their points, but rather, just be focused on getting the movements in safely. 

So in closing of this ridiculously long novel, lol, which I think most of my reviews tend to be, which can kind of suck for people who start getting bored lol, I would say that this is a game I heartily recommend. I did not experience bugs/glitching and it was smooth, enjoyable and also effective. 
I’m not sure how intense it gets as you play on for weeks, but I will say that I definitely recommend this for beginners and those that have physical limitations and possibly even those with some neurodivergent mental limitations as well, because I think it’s enough to grab people‘s attention and easily focus on the areas that you have to target. If I were still a trainer, I would be recommending this to all of the individuals I’d train with all of the different issues that make exercise challenging for them. And even now, I will be recommending to folks as well!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to write such a thorough and insightful review of our game! As former fitness trainer, your perspective on accessibility and special populations is particularly valuable. We're delighted to hear that you've found joy in both the gameplay mechanics and our characters (especially our snarky Demonomicon!).

Your feedback about creating a dedicated seated mode is especially thoughtful, and we appreciate you highlighting how this could benefit players with different mobility needs. We're also taking note of your suggestions about the movement space during dodge sections.

We created this game to make fitness more accessible and enjoyable for everyone, so it's particularly rewarding to hear that we're helping players who might find other VR fitness apps too intense. Your suggestions for improvements will help us make the experience even better for our community.

Thank you again for your detailed feedback and for recommending our game to others. We're committed to making fitness fun and accessible for all players, and reviews like yours help guide us in the right direction.

reviews.review_description: This game really got me moving which is exactly what we needed in a workout game. The music was pleasant and who doesn't love beating up demons, I know I do. Recommend for those who wants to get a workout and having fun doing it.

Clever and interesting idea for a VR workout game.  Very original and unique. Well made and very polished experience. Great characters and voice acting.  
A portal opens up in your room with mixed reality. A Demon army is invading your world to take over and you’re the only one that can stop them.  
Punching or dodging targets. Some focus on timing.(I would prefer hitting the demons over the targets)
It’s broken up a bit with dodging and collecting coin sections. 
Buy different fists and cosmetics for the trainers with the coins. Earn soulbars from workouts to gift to the trainers for more story and interactions.
Daily challenges to keep you coming back. 
I really do like everything about this game, but think the gameplay is missing something or a little too limited to be a top contender. 
I do enjoy it and get a good workout.  So it will definitely be added to my rotation.

Not like any other workout game!!!

The core of this game is fun. I like the characters and art style. Also, the name of the game is a great pun! The core concept is pretty good, as it's like one of those punching workout games, but it's actually trying to be fun and not just relying on music for entertainment.

Unfortunately it's killed by repetition. In the main menu, the four main demon characters have only a few things to say. They were funny and cute the first few times I heard them, but it was a short loop. I soon got tired of them. 

The gameplay is fun, but it's essentially the same every time. You "fight" some invading demons, which are only mild variations of each other. They also have a very short roster of things to say. Then some flying demons come at you, which you either punch or avoid. Then you duck around, avoiding floating obstacles and collecting coins. Then you are in a new location, but it's basically the same as the online location. It has no impact on gameplay. You repeat this a few times and you are done.

There's a progressing story in the game, but that story is separate from the gameplay. You complete a course and get a soulbar then you go into a different place from the main menu to feed the soulbar to your trainer. Do that enough times, you get an animated story section. Then you go back to the game to get more soulbars. It's a little strange that they didn't integrate the story into the gameplay.

You can also spend the coins you collect while playing, but it's mostly cosmetics. Nothing I've seen actually changes the game. You can buy soul bars to progress the story a little faster, but I think that's it.

I'm hoping they can add some more content to the game, maybe add some boss fights, and make it so the story and the gameplay are intertwined. If they do that, I think this will be a fun way to get some movement!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for this detailed and thoughtful review! You've given us a lot to think about, especially around variety and story integration. This is just version 1.0, and we're already brainstorming ways to expand and improve the core experience.

We definitely hear you on the repetitive dialogue (and yeah, we probably should have recorded a few more main menu barks - lesson learned! 😅). We actually might have some unused recorded lines we can add in soon to help mix things up a bit.

The integration between story and gameplay is something we're particularly focused on as we plan our next steps. Your suggestions about boss fights and better story/gameplay connection are really helpful as we figure out where to take the game next.

Thank you for being an early adopter and taking the time to write such a comprehensive review. 

We have lots more fun stuff planned for the future - stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
If you like fitness and rhythm style games and a Saturday morning cartoon vibe, then there is a good chance you will like this. It’s a fun game with interesting characters! It is unique having a story in a fitness style game. Works well as the story elements disguise the fitness part with fun gameplay. 

Looking forward to more updates! Keep up the good work.

this game is a major improvement from your last game God of Riffs, having fun mechanics and hitting the demons with a jab than  a left or right jab than finishing with an uppercut, and the characters are great like frun and the trainers like ash or zephyr  which have unique abilities, and the shop giving you the ability to customize your gloves or the trainers clothes. what I don get is if you lightly hit the demons you  cant tell if I hit them or they hurt me, the solution would to make the vibration about the same has hitting them hard 
but this game is major step up 

great work VYERSOFT 👍

reviews.review_title: Fun game that really gets you sweating!
reviews.review_description:
This game is a great way to workout while having fun. Punching blocks to defeat enemies and squatting and dodging obstacles and enemies was pretty fun. 

The graphics were charming with friendly and witty demon trainers and evil skeleton enemies. I thought the voice-acting was spot on and the story was quite engaging, making me want to co time playing to progress the story. Overall, a pretty good game for the price.
